column12: 10 MCAQs (which includes Aptitude, programming and sql), 1 programming question, 1 SQL question to solve.

column9: HR only focused on asking questions related to ME. She wanted to know more about ME. My background, how i am suitable for the job, how i can handle both Mtech and work together. She asked me about my motivation and what i learnt from my internship. She also asked questions related to the company - their competitors, clients and what they do.
column10: String question - to compare two strings and return a result removing the common characters from both strings. They asked question's related to my projects. Why I used that particular tech stack. They also asked questions related to dbms (constraints and indexing).
column11: We could use any programming language. There was one coding question (longest path in the grid), one sql query question and around 10 mcq questions. Mcq's were related to time complexity, finding errors in the code and choosing the correct queries that return the given output.

column9: It was really a great experience. In HR Round, they will mostly ask questions about yourself and they will also check how much you know about the company. Additionally, they might give some scenario based questions. Sample questions: 1. Tell me about yourself apart from what you have mentioned in the resume 2. What do you know about our company? 3. How do you handle stress? 4. Tell me a time when you worked as a team 5. Tell me about your project 6. Why do you want to join our company only? 7. Why should we hire you? 8.(Scenario-based) Three of you in a team are working in a project. Your half work is completed. Meanwhile i member leaves the team and your deadline is reaching soon. What three approaches will you use to complete your project on time 9. Where do you see yourself in five years

column11: "01110011010" This is the pattern. You have to print the maximum times consecutive 1's are there in pattern. Use java and write full code. eg. in this pattern "111" has max consecutive one's so o/p should be 3.
